meta.title: Filter Base

meta.title: Accumulation points
meta.excerpt: Let $A\subset X$ and $x\in X$. The point $x$ is called accumulation point if for any Neighbourhood $U$ of $x$ we have $(A\setminus\{x\})\cap U\neq\emptyset$. The derived set $A'$ is the set of accumulation points of $A$.

meta.title: Connectedness
meta.excerpt: Let $X$ be a topological space. We say it is disconnected if we can find open $U$ and $V$ s.t. $U\cap V=\emptyset$, $U\cup V=X$ and they are nonempty. We say $X$ is connected if it is not disconnected. The image of a con

meta.title: Continuity in topological spaces
meta.excerpt: Let $f:X\to Y$ where $X$ and $Y$ are topological spaces. Then $f$ is continuous if the preimage of every open set in $Y$ is open in $X$. Lemma Equivalently, $f$ is cts iff preimage of every closed set in $Y$ is closed in

meta.title: Continuous
meta.excerpt: Let $\mathcal{U},\mathcal{V}$ be Topological Spaces We say that $f:\mathcal{U}\to \mathcal{V}$ is continuous if for any open $V\subseteq \mathcal{V}$: $$ f^{-1}(V)=\{ u\in \mathcal{U} : f(u)\in V \} $$ is open in $\mathc

meta.title: Dense
meta.excerpt: Let $X$ be a Topological Space. Let $D\subseteq X$. Let $x\in X$. Suppose that any open Neighbourhood $U$ of $x$ has $U\cap D\neq \varnothing$. Then $D$ is dense at $x$. Additionally we say that $D$ is dense in $X$ if $D

meta.title: Hausdorff
meta.excerpt: Let $X$ be a Topological Space Suppose any two $x,y\in X$ have open sets $U$ and $V$ such that: - $x\in U,\ y\in V$ - $U\cap V=\emptyset$ Then $X$ is called Hausdorff. Any Metric Space is Hausdorff.

meta.title: Heine-Borel Theorem
meta.excerpt: A subset $K\subseteq \mathbb R^n$ is compact if and only if it is closed and bounded. Proof Suppose $K$ is compact. Then $f:K\to\mathbb R$ by $f(x)=||x||$ is bounded, so $K$ is bounded. Furthermore, $K$ is closed in Haus

meta.title: Homeomorphism
meta.excerpt: A function $f:X\to Y$ between Topological Spaces is a homeomorphism if: - $f$ is bijective - $f$ is continuous - $f^{-1}$ is continuous

meta.title: Idempotent Lemma
meta.excerpt: Let $X$ be a nonempty Compact Hausdorff Topological space. Let $+:X\times X\to X$ be left-Continuous and Associative. Then there is some $x\in X$ with $x+x=x$. Proof

meta.title: Interior
meta.excerpt: Let $A$ be a subset of a Topological Space $X$. We define $$ A^{o}=\bigcup{U\subseteq A\text{ open}} U $$ to be the interior of $A$. Lemma $$ A^{o} = \{ x\in X: A\text{ is a nbd of }x \} $$ and $A^{o}$ is open.

meta.title: Neighbourhood
meta.excerpt: For $x\in X$ and $U\subset X$, then $U$ is the neighbourhood of $x$ if there is an open $V\subset X$ s.t. $x\in V\subset U$.

meta.title: Open map
meta.excerpt: $f:X\to Y$ is an open map if it maps open sets in $X$ to open sets in $Y$

meta.title: Quotient space
meta.excerpt: Let $(X,\tau)$ be a topological space and $R$ an equivalence relation on $X$. Define the quotient map $q:X\to X/R$ with $q(x)=[x]$. Equip this space with the quotient topology: $$\tauR=\{V\subset X/R: q^{-1}(V)\in\tau\}$

meta.excerpt: $X$ is semi locally simply connected if for all $x \in X$ there is an open $U{x}$ neighbourhood of $x$ st any loop in $U{x}$ based at $x$ is contractible in $X$.
